title: Xylocarpus rumphii
taxonomy: Xylocarpus rumphii is classified as a species of tree within the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family. This family consists of approximately 350 species of trees and shrubs, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Xylocarpus rumphii is native to the Indian Ocean and Pacific regions, including parts of Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. It is a member of the genus Xylocarpus, which comprises three species of mangrove trees that are adapted to living in coastal saline environments. As a member of the family Meliaceae, Xylocarpus rumphii is also related to other species of trees, such as the mahogany (Swietenia macrophylla) and the obeche (Lovoa trichilioides).
uses: Xylocarpus rumphii is found to have various medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Its bark and leaves are also believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections. Furthermore, the plant is used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, its extracts have shown potential in reducing inflammation and pain.
benefits: Xylocarpus rumphii, a mangrove tree species native to the tropical regions of the Indo-Pacific, has been found to possess various health benefits. The tree's bark, in particular, is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body, potentially alleviating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, the tree's leaves have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Xylocarpus rumphii.

constituents: Xylocarpus rumphii, a plant species in the family Meliaceae, has been found to contain various bioactive compounds. The active constituents of Xylocarpus rumphii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. These compounds have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Xylocarpus rumphii, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to exhibit strong antioxidant properties. intro: Xylocarpus rumphii, also known as the mangrove apple or mangrove fig, is a species of mangrove tree native to the tropical coasts of Asia and Australia. The health benefits of Xylocarpus rumphii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds, fever, and respiratory infections.
